response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced Staff/Senior Software Engineer with over 15 years of professional experience, making him severely overqualified for an entry-level Junior Java Developer role. More critically, his German is self-described as only 'Basic,' which disqualifies him from a position that explicitly requires strong verbal and written German for client-facing work in Germany. While Java appears in his skills list and he has used it in specific past roles, his primary expertise is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — Java is clearly not his main language. His extensive experience, though impressive, would make this role a non-starter from both a seniority fit and language requirement standpoint.
strengths: Computer Science degree and deep, multi-disciplinary OOP experience across Java, TypeScript, C++, and others
Demonstrated agile practices including TDD, CI/CD, pairing, and trunk-based development
Cloud experience (AWS CDK) and full-stack capabilities relevant to digitalization projects
concerns: Massively overqualified — currently a Staff Software Engineer with 15+ years of experience applying for an entry-level role
German listed as 'Basic,' which is a hard disqualifier for a role requiring strong written and verbal German for client collaboration
Java is a peripheral skill; primary expertise is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, not the Java stack central to this role
